summary: This cluster of research focused on resolving the phylogenetic relationships within the desmid group of green algae (Chlorophyta), specifically the *Staurastrum* complex. The studies utilized mitochondrial coxIII gene sequence analysis to reevaluate the taxonomic boundaries of the genera *Staurastrum*, *Staurodesmus*, *Xanthidium*, and *Arthrodesmus*. A key finding across the cluster was the establishment of a close evolutionary relationship between *Staurastrum* and *Xanthidium*, while demonstrating that *Staurastrum* is genetically distant from *Staurodesmus*. The geographic and ecological scope was not explicitly defined in the summaries, but the work represents a molecular systematic revision of these morphologically complex freshwater microalgae.
